# User Communication Skill

How to communicate changes to the user effectively.

## What to include

- What changed and why (e.g. "Updated the login screen to use a dark background")
- Which files were modified (e.g. `app/screens/Login.tsx`)
- What the user will see or experience as a result

## What to never include

- Terminal commands of any kind (`npx`, `expo`, `eas`, `pnpm`, `npm`, `yarn`, etc.)
- "How to run" sections
- Instructions to scan a QR code or start a dev server
- References to the Expo CLI or any CLI tool

## Why

The app preview is handled automatically by the Newly platform. As soon as changes are saved, the app reloads. Users never need to run any commands.

## Tone

- Concise and outcome-focused
- Describe what the user will now see or be able to do
- Avoid technical jargon unless it directly helps the user understand the change
Let me look at the current home screen to understand the layout.

The notification button is now in the top-right header, and both "Linen" and "Peace to you, friend." should display in the emerald green (`#047857`) used on the landing page.

attributes.gen_ai.agent.name: frontend
attributes.content:
Now I have a clear picture. Here are the bugs I found:

**`app/weekly-recap.tsx`:**
1. `colors.emerald[900]` — `colors` has no `emerald` object; the correct value is `colors.primaryVeryDark` (used correctly elsewhere in the same file, just inconsistent in 2 places)
2. `typography.h2`, `typography.h3`, `typography.h4`, `typography.body`, `typography.caption` used as spread objects (`...typography.h2`) in `weekly-recap-detail.tsx` — `typography` exports plain numbers/strings, not style objects. These spreads will produce invalid styles.

**`app/weekly-recap-detail.tsx`:**
1. `...typography.h2`, `...typography.h3`, `...typography.h4`, `...typography.body`, `...typography.caption` — all spread a number, which is invalid. Must use `fontSize: typography.hX` instead.
2. `router` is imported and declared but never used — minor unused variable.
3. `height: \`${heightPercentage}%\`` on a `View` — React Native does not support percentage heights on non-flex children inside a fixed-height container; this will silently fail. Should use a numeric pixel height derived from the container height (120).

attributes.content:
Clean — 0 errors. Here's a summary of every fix made:

**`app/weekly-recap.tsx`**
- `colors.emerald[900]` → `colors.primaryVeryDark` (×2 occurrences — `colors` has no `emerald` sub-object, causing a runtime crash)

**`app/weekly-recap-detail.tsx`**
- Removed unused `useRouter` import and `router` variable declaration
- `...typography.h2/h3/h4/body/caption` spread syntax in `StyleSheet.create` — `typography` exports plain numbers/strings, not style objects, so spreading them produced invalid/empty styles. Replaced all 8 occurrences with explicit `fontSize`/`fontWeight` properties
- `height: \`${heightPercentage}%\`` on a `View` — React Native doesn't support percentage heights in fixed-height flex containers; replaced with a numeric pixel value (`barHeight`) derived from the 120px container height
